title: MedsCheck Forms
overview: A comprehensive web and desktop application for Ontario pharmacists to complete all 6 government-mandated MedsCheck forms with AI-powered clinical note extraction, Supabase backend, and instant PDF generation.
features: AI-powered data extraction — upload images (OCR) or paste clinical notes, auto-populates patient demographics, medications, allergies, and conditions via Google Gemini API

Complete coverage of all 6 MedsCheck forms — Healthcare Provider Notification, Patient Acknowledgement, Personal Medication Record, Pharmacist Worksheet, Patient Take-Home Summary, Diabetes Education Checklist
Supabase backend — full PostgreSQL persistence, user authentication, patient management, multi-pharmacy support, auto-save
Tauri-powered desktop app — lightweight Windows .exe (~8MB) with dual-mode database (cloud Supabase or local SQLite), offline capability, and optional cloud sync
Professional PDF generation — live preview, download individual or all 6 forms, digital signature support (draw or upload)
Smart logic — conditional Diabetes Education form auto-hides for non-diabetic patients, bi-directional profile sync, intelligent data persistence
Modern interface — tab-based workflow, dark/light mode, responsive 50/50 split layout, real-time PDF preview
CI/CD — GitHub Actions auto-builds and packages a signed Windows .exe on every push to main
challenges: Building a dual-mode database adapter that seamlessly switches between cloud Supabase and local SQLite, translating PostgreSQL schemas with RLS policies into a secure offline-first SQLite architecture, and implementing AI-powered clinical note extraction that reliably parses unstructured pharmacist notes into structured form fields.
outcome: A production-grade tool used by Ontario pharmacists with full form coverage, AI extraction, professional PDF output, and a standalone desktop application with offline capability.
